2605.12586.title: 3D Primitives are a Spatial Language for VLMs
2605.12586.paper_summary: Vision-language models (VLMs) exhibit a striking paradox: they can generate executable code that reconstructs a 3D scene from geometric primitives with correct object counts, classes, and approximate positions, yet the same models fail at simpler spatial questions on the same image. We show that 3D geometric primitives (cubes, spheres, cylinders, expressed in executable code) serve as a powerful intermediate representation for spatial understanding, and exploit this through three contributions. First, we introduce \textbf{\textsc{SpatialBabel}}, a benchmark evaluating fourteen VLMs on primitive-based 3D scene reconstruction across six \emph{scene-code languages} (programming languages and declarative formats for 3D primitive scenes), revealing that a single model's object-detection F1 can vary by up to $5.7\times$ across languages. Second, we propose \textbf{Code-CoT} (Code Chain-of-Thought), a training-free inference strategy that routes spatial reasoning through primitive-based code generation. Code-CoT lifts the SpatialBabel-QA-Score by up to $+6.4$\% on primitive scenes and real-photo CV-Bench-3D accuracy by $+5.0$\% for VLMs with strong coding capabilities. Third, we propose \textbf{S$^{3}$-FT} (Self-Supervised Spatial Fine-Tuning), which self-supervisedly distills primitive spatial knowledge into general visual reasoning by parsing the model's own Three.js primitive-reconstructions into structured annotations and fine-tuning on the result, with \emph{no human labels and no teacher model}. Training on primitive images alone, S$^3$-FT improves Qwen3-VL-8B by $+4.6$ to $+8.6$\% on SpatialBabel-Primitive-QA, $+9.7$\% on CV-Bench-2D, and $+17$\% on HallusionBench; the recipe transfers across model families. These results establish geometric primitives in code as both a diagnostic and a transferable spatial vocabulary for VLMs. We will release all artifacts upon publication.
2605.12586.paper_summary_zh: 視覺語言模型（VLMs）展現出一個引人注目的悖論：它們能夠生成可執行的代碼，從幾何原始體重建三維場景，並正確計算物體數量、類別和近似位置，但同樣的模型在同一圖像上卻無法解答更簡單的空間問題。我們顯示，三維幾何原始體（立方體、球體、圓柱體，以可執行代碼表示）作為空間理解的強大中介表示，並通過三項貢獻來利用這一點。首先，我們介紹了 \textbf{\textsc{SpatialBabel}}，這是一個基準，評估十四個 VLMs 在基於原始體的三維場景重建中的表現，涵蓋六種 \emph{場景代碼語言}（用於三維原始體場景的編程語言和聲明格式），顯示單一模型的物體檢測 F1 在不同語言之間的變化可達 $5.7\times$。其次，我們提出了 \textbf{Code-CoT}（代碼思維鏈），這是一種無需訓練的推理策略，通過基於原始體的代碼生成來引導空間推理。Code-CoT 在原始場景上將 SpatialBabel-QA-Score 提升了最多 $+6.4$\%，並在具有強大編碼能力的 VLMs 上將真實照片 CV-Bench-3D 的準確率提高了 $+5.0$\%。第三，我們提出了 \textbf{S$^{3}$-FT}（自我監督空間微調），它通過將模型自身的 Three.js 原始體重建解析為結構化註釋，自我監督地提煉原始空間知識為一般視覺推理，並在結果上進行微調，\emph{無需人類標籤和教師模型}。僅在原始圖像上訓練，S$^3$-FT 在 SpatialBabel-Primitive-QA 上將 Qwen3-VL-8B 的表現提高了 $+4.6$ 到 $+8.6$\%，在 CV-Bench-2D 上提高了 $+9.7$\%，在 HallusionBench 上提高了 $+17$\%；這一方法在不同模型家族之間可轉移。這些結果確立了代碼中的幾何原始體作為 VLMs 的診斷工具和可轉移的空間詞彙。我們將在發表時釋出所有文檔。
2605.12586.author: Junze Liu et.al.
2605.12586.authors: Junze Liu, Kun Qian, Florian Dubost, Kai Zhong, Arvind Srinivasan, Nan Chen, Anping Wang, Sam Zhang, Alejandro Mottini, Qingjun Cui, Tian Wang
